operating_state: Total revenues increased to $1.769 billion driven by the Duke Transaction acquisition completed in October 2022, adding 144 million square feet of industrial properties. Consolidated rental revenues rose to $1.634 billion with same-store NOI growth of 9.9% driven by strong rent change on rollover of 68.8% and sustained 98.0% occupancy. Core FFO increased to $1.157 billion from $834 million year-over-year, while net earnings declined to $463 million from $1.149 billion due to lower gains on property dispositions.
cost_structure: Real Estate Segment NOI expanded reflecting Duke portfolio integration and positive rental rate growth. Rental expenses increased due to higher insurance costs from an active storm season and the expanded asset base. Real estate depreciation and amortization rose reflecting the larger property portfolio. G&A expenses increased due to inflationary pressures and performance-based compensation, partially offset by capitalizing development-related internal costs.
material_events: The company issued $2.6 billion of senior notes during the quarter with a weighted average interest rate of 4.5%, including euro-denominated and U.S. dollar debt. In April 2023, Prologis amended and upsized its global credit facility by $1.0 billion to $6.5 billion total capacity. Development activity remained limited with only 2 new building starts, while 15 buildings stabilized with 6.5% weighted average yield. Quarterly dividends increased to $0.87 per common share from $0.79.
macro_exposures: The company faces foreign currency fluctuation exposure across international investments, experiencing unrealized losses compared to prior year gains. Interest rate risk increased given new debt issuances at 4.5% weighted average rate compared to the portfolio's 2.6% rate. Current market conditions have led to anticipated declines in asset valuations, prompting management caution in capital deployment.
forward_outlook: Management expects increased development starts and contributions to open-ended funds in the second half of 2023 as market conditions stabilize. The company maintains $5.7 billion liquidity and anticipates continued organic revenue growth driven by substantial lease mark-to-market opportunity. Strategic capital business growth is planned through increased assets under management, while recent debt proceeds will fund general corporate purposes including debt repayment.
uncertainty_profile: The company operates in an uncertain macroeconomic environment affecting contribution activity, property disposition timing, and real estate valuations. Remaining equity commitments require fulfillment through property contributions or cash. Development portfolio completion requires additional investment with properties currently under-leased. Foreign currency translation adjustments continue affecting comprehensive income through international investments.
